verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a lysosomal-resident ABC transporter with 10 transmembrane helices. The N-terminal TMD0 domain contains the lysosomal targeting signal and is essential for lysosomal localization; deletion of this domain causes mislocalization to plasma membrane, confirming that active targeting to lysosomes excludes PM residence. Direct experimental evidence confirms TAPL is NOT detected on the plasma membrane: surface biotinylation is negative, dynamin inhibition does not accumulate it at PM, and it is absent from bone marrow-derived dendritic cells. Full-length protein traffics exclusively to lysosomes via ER→Golgi→early endosome→lysosome route. No documented stress-induced, activation-induced, cell-death-induced, or tissue-restricted surface display. Not a candidate for cell-surface-directed therapeutics.
